topic: Iran Launches Missile Strike on Saudi Oil Fields
summary: Iran has launched a major ballistic missile attack targeting Saudi Arabia's eastern oil infrastructure, including the Abqaiq processing facility. Multiple explosions reported across the Eastern Province. Oil futures surging 15% in pre-market trading. The UN Security Council has called an emergency session.
left_narrative: Left-leaning outlets emphasize the civilian toll and call for immediate de-escalation. They frame this as a consequence of the Trump administration's withdrawal from the Iran nuclear deal, arguing diplomatic channels were abandoned. CNN and MSNBC highlight refugee displacement and humanitarian corridors.
right_narrative: Right-leaning media frames the strike as proof that Obama-era appeasement emboldened Iran. Fox News and Daily Wire call for a decisive military response and criticize Biden-era diplomacy as weakness. They emphasize the threat to US energy independence and national security.
what_they_arent_telling_you: Neither side is discussing Saudi Arabia's quiet military buildup along the Yemeni border in the weeks prior, or the $12B arms deal signed with a US defense contractor just 10 days ago. The timing suggests this escalation may have been anticipated — and possibly welcomed — by defense industry stakeholders on both sides.
social_summary: Twitter is flooded with unverified footage. #IranAttack and #WWIII are trending. Crypto markets spiking as a 'safe haven' narrative takes hold. Conspiracy theories about false flag operations are already circulating on Telegram and TikTok.
image_prompt: A dramatic night sky over an industrial oil facility with distant orange explosions on the horizon, smoke billowing against a dark blue sky, cinematic wide shot
